t_ch: 等到什么时候啊！
t_en: Until when!?

t_ch: 就是说啊！管饭吗？
t_en: Right!? What do we eat in the meantime?

t_ch: 大家都很忙！
t_en: Everyone is busy!

t_ch: 稍安勿躁，理解万岁！
t_en: Let's stay calm, mutual respect is key!

t_ch: 不好意思，能否占用下您宝贵的时间，就只是做个简单的新闻采访！请放心，不会耽误您多少时间的，就是几个问题而已！
t_en: Excuse me, could you spare a moment of your time for a quick interview? It's only a few questions and won't take up too much time!

t_ch: 忘了自我介绍，我是《曜青体育》驻竞锋舰记者馥林，《曜青体育》是注册的官方媒体。
t_en: Ah, you must be wondering who I am. I'm Fulin, a correspondent on the Skysplitter from Yaoqing Sports, an official media partner.

t_ch: 好吧，那你问吧。
t_en: Sure, ask away.

t_ch: 真是太感谢了！
t_en: Thank you so much!

t_ch: 那个…我不看比赛？
t_en: Um... I don't watch competitive sports.

t_ch: 没关系的，不看比赛，也有可以问的。
t_en: No problem. I have a separate list of questions for interviewees who don't watch competitive sports.

t_ch: 都是机密，无可奉告。
t_en: No comment. I'm not at liberty to say anything.

t_ch: 哎呀别那么死板嘛，只需要告诉我不踩红线的部分就行。
t_en: Chill, my friend! Don't be so uptight. You can just feed me information that doesn't break the rules.

t_ch: 先来第一个问题，你知道之前飞霄将军大破步离人阴谋的事吗？
t_en: Let's start off with this: Do you know of the incident whereby General Feixiao foiled the borisin's nefarious plans?

t_ch: 其实我是当事人。
t_en: Yes, I was actually involved in it.

t_ch: 那也太好了吧！
t_en: Whoa, I hit the jackpot!

t_ch: 完全不知道，别问我……
t_en: Not at all. Don't ask me about it...

t_ch: 那请你假装自己是当事人，拜托了……
t_en: Oh... Then can you pretend that you were involved in it? Please...!

t_ch: 从你的角度看，那群步离人为什么会登上竞锋舰？
t_en: Why do you think the borisin boarded the Skysplitter?

t_ch: 我不知道，你去问步离人？
t_en: I don't know. Try asking the borisin?

t_ch: 哦，步离人也不知道自己为什么要登上竞锋舰。
t_en: Oh, they don't know what made them do it either.

t_ch: 是将军的计谋引导至此。
t_en: It was all part of the general's plan.

t_ch: 嗐，读者想看的并不是这种四平八稳「包饺子」的剧情。
t_en: Eh, uninspiring happily-ever-afters like this aren't going to catch any reader's interest.

t_ch: 他们应该是为了逃走！
t_en: They were probably trying to run away!

t_ch: 对，他们确实是为了逃走，但是这没有一点反转，没劲！
t_en: Yes, they were indeed trying to escape... But where's the plot twist!? Booooring!

t_ch: 那这样吧…「飞霄将军在一系列事件中被云骑军追杀，她一边防备一边追查，原来步离人早就渗透了云骑军高层，而竞锋舰其实是步离人运作几十年的邪恶计划结果。」
t_en: How about this: "Even as the Cloud Knights relentlessly hunted her down in a series of incidents, General Feixiao remained steadfast, leaving no stone unturned in her search for the truth as she stayed on her guard. It turns out the borisin had long since infiltrated the top-level Cloud Knights. The Skysplitter was, in fact, the culmination of the diabolical plans that the borisin had been scheming over the last few decades."

t_ch: 这种内容真的没问题吗？
t_en: Are you sure that content won't get you in trouble?

t_ch: 这种程度就是一般的体育八卦，热点嘛！大家都知道它不是真的。
t_en: Nah, it's nothing beyond the standard of usual gossip in the world of sports. It's just clickbait! Everybody knows not to take it seriously.

t_ch: 假新闻！我不接受采访了。
t_en: That's fake news! This interview is over.

t_ch: 哎，这个嘛，只是在真实内容上稍微增加一点点故事性而已。
t_en: Eh, I'm just embellishing the truth a little.

t_ch: 你放心，没事的，这都是常规操作。你帮我完成采访，我也会给你提供一些方便的，咱们是互惠互利。你知道媒体舆论吗？你想要的我都有……
t_en: Relax, it'll be fine. It's all part of the routine. If you help me with the interview, I'll provide you assistance in other ways. It's a win-win situation. I'm sure you've heard of how the media can influence public opinion, right? I can give you everything you dream of...

t_ch: 哟，我正在考虑报道的事呢。怎么，需要一些舆论服务？
t_en: Why, hello! I was just thinking about my next article. So, are you looking for help with influencing public opinion?

t_ch: 有些事想问你。
t_en: I have a few questions to ask you...

t_ch: 你写的虚假报道没问题吗？
t_en: Is the misleading article gonna land you in trouble?

t_ch: 没有，完全没有。只要能取一个*醒目*的标题，一切都会迎刃而解。
t_en: Not at all. An attention-grabbing headline can solve everything.

t_ch: 这不就是标题党？
t_en: That's just clickbait, isn't it?

t_ch: 我大概能理解的。
t_en: I kind of get it.

t_ch: 设想一下，当你点开手机，看到新闻推送的时候。除了配图之外，映入眼帘的第一眼信息是什么？
t_en: Think about it: When you see a push notification for a piece of news on your phone, what's the first thing you see, apart from the accompanying image?

t_ch: 就是标题。取什么样的标题，会决定用户先点进哪条新闻里。
t_en: The article's headline, right? See, the headline decides which article a user opens and reads first.

t_ch: 如果没有抓眼球的标题，阅读量就低，数据就差，没人会给数据差的文章推送。接着就是栏目就会被裁撤，你的饭碗也就保不住。
t_en: If you don't compose an attention-grabbing headline, your readership drops, the statistics fall, and the algorithms stop pushing articles with low click numbers to users. Next thing you know, your column is axed, and you're out of a job.

t_ch: 再说了，如果读者获取不了情绪价值，也不会有人愿意去自主传播。
t_en: Besides, if the reader didn't derive any emotional value from the article, they wouldn't spread the news on their own accord either.

t_ch: 那些骂我是标题党的读者，在批判我之前，还是先想想自己会点开什么标题吧！
t_en: Before lambasting me, those that despise me for writing clickbait should do some self-reflection and think about what kind of headline they'd click on in the first place!

t_ch: 感觉比起记者，你更适合小说。
t_en: You should be a novelist instead of a journalist.

t_ch: 唉，平心而论，我自己也无数次的考虑过是否换行。
t_en: *sigh* Honestly, I've toyed with the idea of a career change more times than I can count.

t_ch: 我曾经的梦想就是写剑侠话本。然而现在这个年头，连载平台上的剑侠作品没多少人打榜，让做专职作家是不可能的了。于是，我加入了《曜青体育》。
t_en: It was my dream once upon a time to write a swordplay epic, but such works don't receive much support on serialization platforms anymore. A job as a full-time novelist is just impossible nowadays. That's why I joined Yaoqing Sports.

t_ch: 一开始我也是一本正经的追求着报道真实，但数据惨的一塌糊涂。
t_en: When I first started out, I was a proper journalist who cared about accurate reporting too. Unfortunately, the analytics for my work was absolutely dismal.

t_ch: 后来我接了一篇关于赌拳赛打假拳的报道。我没采访到参与者，就把打假拳的过程杜撰了一下，结果反倒大受好评。
t_en: Later, I took on a gig pursuing a lead about betting on fixed boxing matches. I couldn't score any interviews with the parties involved, so I cooked up a story about the match-fixing process. Guess what? People just lapped it up!

t_ch: 大家都知道我是编的故事，但是大家就是想看——你明白了吗？这和小说其实也没有太多差别了，那也不必换行了。
t_en: Everybody knew the article was a lie, but they couldn't help but want to read it. You see what I mean, don't you? This isn't actually that different from writing novels. In that case, there's no point in a career switch anymore.

t_ch: 所以你提供的舆论服务正经吗？
t_en: So, are your public opinion services reliable?

t_ch: 哦，这件事咱们不能大声地说，不过效果你是可以放心的。
t_en: Ahem! These things aren't meant to be discussed so openly, but you needn't worry about the outcome.

t_ch: 「敌人的丑闻」？
t_en: "Enemy's scandals"?

t_ch: 没错，击倒一名强大的敌人，往往不需要什么兵刃，而是一条丑闻。
t_en: Uh-huh. You don't always need to draw steel against a powerful enemy to take them down. A scandal can do the trick too.

t_ch: 「吹捧的通稿」？
t_en: "Laudatory press releases"?

t_ch: 你知道的，要是通稿足够多，很多事情都会很方便。
t_en: You know how it goes: Sing the praises of somebody enough times, and life will naturally be made easier for them.

t_ch: 「水军」？
t_en: "Paid bots"?

t_ch: 是的，网络昵称背后又不都是真人，但是他们混在真人之中。
t_en: That's right. Not everyone behind their net alias is a real individual, and these bots are mixed in with those that are.

t_ch: 这不太好吧！
t_en: That doesn't sound like the right thing to do!

t_ch: 这确实不好，我承认。
t_en: Indeed, it isn't.

t_ch: 所以，你得道德败坏，才不会良心不安。想清楚了，再找我。
t_en: That's why you have to discard your moral values, so you won't feel the prick of conscience. You can come back again when you've made up your mind.

t_ch: 先不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll stop bothering you for now.

t_ch: 好啊，慢走，回头记得关注我在《曜青体育》上的专栏。
t_en: Sure. See you around, and don't forget to follow my column in Yaoqing Sports!

t_ch: 先不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll stop bothering you for now.

t_ch: 好啊，慢走，回头记得关注我在《曜青体育》上的专栏。
t_en: Sure. See you around, and don't forget to follow my column in Yaoqing Sports!

t_ch: 说说你能给我什么好处来着？
t_en: What benefits can you offer?

t_ch: 你想要的通稿、黑稿、水军，我应有尽有……
t_en: Mass-published press releases, disparaging articles, bots... You name it, I have it.

t_ch: 我这次出航，不知道要多久才会回来了…即使要两百年，你也会等我吗？
t_en: I'm not sure how long I'll be away for this time... Perhaps two hundred years. Will you wait for me?

t_ch: 我会一直等着你的，亲爱的。
t_en: I'll always wait for you, my dear.

t_ch: 谢谢你，亲爱的。
t_en: Thank you, darling.

t_ch: 亲爱的，我好像感受到充满嫉妒的视线了。
t_en: Darling, I can sense glares of jealousy upon us.

t_ch: 总有人没见过卿卿我我，不要在意{F#她}{M#他}。
t_en: Some people have never witnessed expressions of true love. Don't give {F#her}{M#him} a second thought.

t_ch: 敢在这个当口偷懒的人，我一律罚去配合地衡司…
t_en: If anyone dares slack off at this juncture, I'll send 'em off to the Realm-Keeping Commission...

t_ch: 那岂不是意味着什么脏活累活都要干吗？！
t_en: Does that mean that we have to do all of the dirty work too!?

t_ch: 不要啊！我不想去通下水道！
t_en: No! I don't want to clean the sewers!

t_ch: 仔细想想，为什么声名远扬的大盗会躲在这里打杂呢？这不合理呀。
t_en: Come to think of it, why would an infamous thief be hiding here and doing odd jobs? It doesn't make any sense.

t_ch: …我还是不太能确定一定以及肯定。
t_en: ...I can't absolutely definitely one hundred percent confirm that.

t_ch: 可这家伙长得同星际和平公司通缉令上的肖像一模一样…
t_en: But this person looks exactly like the face on that IPC Wanted poster...

t_ch: 以前这里还不叫「浥尘客栈」。
t_en: This place wasn't always known as the Petrichor Inn.

t_ch: 以前这里叫作什么啊？
t_en: What was this called?

t_ch: 叫「游云逆旅」，你试着念一下吧。
t_en: It was called the Cloud-Striding Hotel for Travelers Near and Far. Go on, try saying that ten times fast.

t_ch: 游云绿绿…
t_en: Cloudy Hotel for Tattlers Neary Far...

t_ch: 游云立…
t_en: Cloudscry Hotel...

t_ch: 幸好它把名字改掉了呀。
t_en: Lucky they changed its name, then.

t_ch: 您是刚到仙舟吗？现在外面不太平，还没找到住处的话，可先到客栈里躲一躲。别担心，老板不收钱的。
t_en: Are you new to the Xianzhou? Things aren't very safe out there at the moment. If you haven't found boarding yet, come and hide out with us in the inn. Don't worry. The boss won't bill you for it.

t_ch: 要登记的是港作拖轮！
t_en: This is a towing skiff for the harbor!

t_ch: 你把被拖的写进去了！
t_en: You wrote down what needs towing away!

t_ch: 都靠自动机巧记录报告多少年了，谁还用脑子来记啊。
t_en: For years, we've been relying on ingenia to record automatically — who still memorizes things manually!?

t_ch: 都说了有东西作祟，工造司都把术算机拖回去维修啦。长点心吧你！
t_en: I told you something was up, and the Artisanship Commission ended up recalling a computational device for repairs. Pay attention!

t_ch: 我们还得擦亮眼睛…
t_en: We should keep our eyes peeled...

t_ch: 要小心别有用心之人…
t_en: Look out for people with ulterior motives...

t_ch: 是啊，尤其是在罗浮上出了这等乱子以后…
t_en: Yeah, especially after all the trouble aboard the Luofu...

t_ch: …你怎么也来了？
t_en: ...What are you doing back here?

t_ch: 司辰宫的偷懒圣地嘛，做个几年大家都会知道这里嘛。
t_en: This is a haven for slackers in the Palace of Astrum. Everyone knows about this place after a few years here.

t_ch: 我这边文件已经堆积成山了，你那边怎么样呢？
t_en: I'm absolutely snowed under with paperwork. How are things with you?

t_ch: 我那边还好，不过嘛，最近驭空大人让我监督一下偷闲的人。
t_en: I'm doing okay, but Madam Yukong has tasked me with checking up on slackers.

t_ch: …你不会是抓我回去的吧。
t_en: ...Have you come to get me?

t_ch: 我也是来偷闲的，放心放心。
t_en: Relax! I came here to slack off too.

t_ch: 你看见悠闲的天舶司职员暗中记下了对方的名字。
t_en: You see the leisurely Sky-Faring Commission staff jotting down the others' names in secret.

t_ch: 威哥说，想在仙舟办好居留手续，就要走后门……
t_en: Wei said that if you want to complete residency procedures on Xianzhou, you may need to go through a back door...

t_ch: 别听他胡说，仙舟司部可是出了名的清廉……
t_en: Don't listen to his nonsense. The Xianzhou government is famously anti-corrupt...

t_ch: 况且人家说「走后门」，也不是让你真的跑到天舶司的后门来啊！你傻啊！
t_en: Besides, when people talk about "back doors," they aren't talking about literal back doors of the Sky-Faring Commission — you idiot!

t_ch: 你说说看，星槎竞赛、狐术幻戏，帝垣琼玉棋…哪个娱乐项目不比呆坐一天，喝一壶淡茶，听人叨叨有意思？
t_en: You tell me: Betting on starskiffs, foxian magic shows, Celestial Jade... Is any of them less amusing than sitting down for an entire day with a pot of watered-down tea while listening to people ramble on?

t_ch: 总有人爱听说书啊，你不喜欢，你算老几？
t_en: There'll always be a market for storytelling. You might not like it, but who are you to criticize it?

t_ch: 你的老家是个什么样的地方？
t_en: What's your hometown like?

t_ch: 老家？哦…你是说我祖先出生的地方……
t_en: My hometown? Well... Do you mean where my ancestors came from...

t_ch: 我的故乡是一个繁荣的矿业星球。在我祖宗的祖宗还年轻时，那里下起了雪，一下就是十年……
t_en: My hometown is a thriving mining planet. Back when my ancestor's ancestor was still young, it started snowing for ten long years...

t_ch: 幸亏那时他在星际和平公司混得还可以，才搞到了最后一张离开的船票……
t_en: Luckily, he had contacts at the IPC, so he managed to get on the last ship out of there...

t_ch: 那后来呢？后来那颗星球怎么样了？
t_en: Then what? What happened to the planet after that?

t_ch: 不知道。我们家已经在仙舟上住了三十多代啦，都算是半个仙舟人了……
t_en: I have no idea. My family has lived on Xianzhou for over thirty generations, and they're all at least part-Xianzhou now.

t_ch: 我真的是狐人！
t_en: I really am a foxian!

t_ch: 你耳朵呢？
t_en: Where are your ears?

t_ch: 得了怪病，耳朵掉了…
t_en: I caught a rare disease and they fell off...

t_ch: 那你尾巴呢？
t_en: What about your tail then?

t_ch: 得了怪病，尾巴掉了…
t_en: I caught a rare disease and it fell off...

t_ch: 哼哼，说漏嘴了吧！其实狐人根本就没有尾巴！
t_en: Ha, you've spilled the beans now! Foxians don't actually have tails!

t_ch: 啊？怎么会这样啊…明明差一点就能混进仙舟，找到长生的秘密了……
t_en: Ah? How can this be... I was so close to stowing aboard the Xianzhou and learning the secret of longevity...

t_ch: 哈哈，上当了吧？狐人是有尾巴的！好啦，不要痴心妄想了，跟我走吧。
t_en: Haha, tricked ya! Foxians DO have tails! Alright, enough wishful dreaming — come this way.

t_ch: 怎么这样啊！你这个骗子！你好过分！
t_en: Huh, how!? You blasted liar, that's too much, man!

t_ch: 我要开大星槎，乙类驾照能开的都不够大！
t_en: I want to pilot a mega-starskiff. Class-B license skiffs aren't big enough for me!

t_ch: 驾驶大型星槎需要甲类驾照。获得甲类驾照需要乙类驾照的驾龄，所以您先开几年民用星槎，就能参加考试……
t_en: A Class-A license is required to pilot mega-starskiffs, and it requires you to have a Class-B license first. So after a few years of piloting civil skiffs, you can take the test...

t_ch: 我不管！我就要开大星槎！
t_en: I don't care. I wanna pilot a mega one!

s_ch: 不再耐心的天舶司职员
t_ch: 罗浮最大，你去把罗浮开走吧。
t_en: The Luofu is the biggest. Go and drive off with it.

t_ch: 诶！你这不是抬杠吗？
t_en: Huh! Are you pulling my leg?

t_ch: 咱俩到底是谁在抬杠？
t_en: Who's pulling whose leg?

t_ch: 新出厂的这艘货运星槎该起什么名字呢…你在学宫是学文学的吧？你出个主意？
t_en: What name should be given to this new freight starskiff... Didn't you take literature lessons back at school? Got any ideas?

t_ch: 嗯…诗云：「昭昭日月明，离离星辰行。」
t_en: Hmm... there is a poem that says: "Bright are the silver moon and the morning sun— Yet life's blessed rest with the star-sown sky."

t_ch: 哦哦，听起来很不错。所以就起名叫做……
t_en: Oh, that sounds lovely. So then it's called...

t_ch: …所以就起名叫做「诗云」吧。
t_en: ...So then let's call it Ode of Clouds then.

t_ch: …你还是去找个医馆看看病吧。
t_en: ...You'd better get to an apothecary and get help.

t_ch: 你好，问您件事，不知道你有没有在竞锋舰上见过一个头上顶着羊角的人？
t_en: Greetings, I have a question requiring your help: Have you seen a person with a ram's horn aboard the Skysplitter?

t_ch: 嗯，好像没有见到过这样的人…
t_en: Hmm, don't think I've seen someone like that...

t_ch: 我也没见到，虽然我从一开始就不觉得真能在线下见面……
t_en: Me neither, though I didn't really expect to meet in-person...

t_ch: 见过，不知道是不是同一个人？
t_en: I have, but I'm not sure if it's the same person?

t_ch: 见过？我估计这里顶着羊角的人也没有第二个了，我怎么没遇到过。
t_en: You have? I doubt there's a second person with a ram's horn stuck on their head, but I haven't seen him.

t_ch: 我再详细地描述一下——头顶着羊角，单片眼镜，戴着火焰做成的冠冕，身披优雅的长袍，洁白缎面的手套，以及很贵的靴子…还有与生俱来的强者气息。
t_en: I shall describe the person in greater detail: It's a ram's horn on top of their head, wearing a monocle, donning a crown of fire, draped in an elegant robe, alabaster-white gloves, and very expensive boots... topped off with the daunting aura of a powerful warrior.

t_ch: 你认识冥火大公？
t_en: You know Duke Inferno?

t_ch: 冥火大公？那不是「泯灭帮」的大人物么，咱们聊的不是一回事。我说的是个名叫「南帝」的网友。
t_en: Duke Inferno? Isn't he THE bigwig from the Annihilation Gang? We're not talking about the same person. I'm looking for an internet friend called "Southern Emperor."

t_ch: 你可能再也见不到了…
t_en: You'll probably never see him again...

t_ch: 哪那么沉重…「南帝」答应过我，说是参加完什么盛会后就见一面。
t_en: Why so serious? Southern Emperor promised me that he'd meet me after some banquet.

t_ch: 那位「南帝」发言极少，但短短几句就让人觉得器宇不凡。我们在暗网约好见上一面，以他的人品来看，不会食言的。
t_en: That Southern Emperor really didn't say much, but I could tell he was extraordinary from those few lines. We said on the dark net that we'd meet up. From his demeanor, I can tell he isn't one to renege on his words.

t_ch: 不过咱也不白来，瞧您这{F#小姐妹}{M#小兄弟}的面相也是一表人才。现在，有一个饿得前胸贴后背的流浪汉站在你面前……
t_en: Not that this was a wasted trip anyway. Just look at you, {F#young miss}{M#young mister}. One glance at your countenance and I can tell you're not an ordinary person. Right now, there's a famished homeless person standing before you...

t_ch: 以您常识来看，是不是应该…适当打发我点什么了？
t_en: Using your common sense, shouldn't it be time to... offer me something?

t_ch: 你好脏，我要走了。
t_en: You're filthy, I'm leaving.

t_ch: 不至于吧，我今天是「清洁模式」。咱是做服务业的，干净卫生的规矩都懂。
t_en: It's not that bad, I'm in clean mode today. Those of us in the service industry are all about hygiene and cleanliness.

t_ch: 连好心人都要骗？
t_en: You trying to fleece someone nice like me?

t_ch: 这您可冤枉我了，您上网看看，咱要饭的过程都是全程录制，保证合法合规。
t_en: Oh come on, just go on the net. We beggars have to record our entire process, we're absolutely legal.

t_ch: 转换话题也太硬了！
t_en: That's not even a segue, it's a non sequitur!

t_ch: 不是，{F#姐}{M#哥}，找网友只是一时的，要饭是一辈子的呀。
t_en: I mean, {F#lady}{M#sir}, internet friends are temporary, but begging for food is forever.

t_ch: 你放心，我不是那种胡搅蛮缠的低端乞丐。我向你保证，情绪价值都有明码标价。
t_en: Don't worry, I'm not one of those pesky low-class beggars. You have my personal guarantee that all emotional values are clearly marked.

t_ch: 呦，怎么说老朋友，要上点情绪价值吗？
t_en: So, how about it, old friend? How about some emotional offerings?

t_ch: 还是随便聊几句吧…
t_en: It's better if we just have a chat...

t_ch: 好嘞，你想聊什么都可以，只要不涉及咱客户们的隐私就成。
t_en: Alright, what d'you want to talk about? Everything's fair game, but let's stay away from client confidentiality stuff.

t_ch: 罗浮仙舟衣食无忧，还有乞丐？
t_en: The Xianzhou Luofu is a proverbial utopia, how are there still beggars?

t_ch: 你说的对。罗浮早就彻底消除了贫困，现在根本没有人需要为了温饱而「乞讨」。所以，这才更有「乞讨」的意义啊！
t_en: You're right. The Luofu has long eliminated poverty, and now absolutely no one has to beg for a warm bed and a hot meal. Which is what makes begging even more meaningful!

t_ch: 一个人如果仅为糊口而写作，那永远成不了「大师」、仅为了生存而盗窃，那永远当不上「大盗」。我这也是同样的道理。
t_en: If someone writes just for a living, he will never be elevated to a master of his profession. If one robs for survival, he will never gain notoriety as a feared bandit. The same principle applies.

t_ch: 如果我是为了温饱而乞讨，那就永远也不是一个…「大丐」？正因为我可以选择「不乞讨」，「乞丐」才谈得上是一种生活方式、一种职业选择、一种毕生志业！
t_en: If I begged for lodging and food, I'd never become a... grand beggar? It is because I can choose not to beg, that being a beggar is a way of life, a profession of choice, and a lifelong pursuit!

t_ch: 你获得满足感的方式好怪。
t_en: Your way of deriving satisfaction is weird.

t_ch: 你误会了，我不缺乏满足感，我是为了罗浮万民的满足感。
t_en: You're mistaken, I do not lack satisfaction, I do it for the satisfaction of the Luofu's citizens.

t_ch: 听起来在消耗大家的善心。
t_en: Sounds like you're just draining everyone's compassion.

t_ch: 此言差矣！我这是为了罗浮万民的满足感。
t_en: That's not true! I do it for the satisfaction of the Luofu's citizens.

t_ch: 动物有互助的本能，但罗浮的生活实在是太美好了。想想看吧！当大家都丰衣足食的时候，到底该如何让自己「互助的本能」得到满足呢？
t_en: Animals instinctively help one another, but life on the Luofu is too perfect. Just think about it! When everyone is fed and clothed, how can they satisfy their instinctual need to help?

t_ch: 诚然，你可以给塔拉萨的希望小学捐款，帮那些勤奋勇敢的小鱼人们游出海沟。但你需要很多年才能看到成效——现代人最没耐心了。
t_en: Indeed, you can make donations to Thalassa's Hope Elementary School, to help those brave little merkids escape the treacherous trench. But that will take many years before you see results. Modern folk require more instant gratification than that.

t_ch: 可如果你选择把钱给我，立刻就能让我吃上一顿好的。而且因为我没有工作，所以我真的需要这笔钱。既然把钱给需要的人就算是行善，那给谁不一样呢？
t_en: But if you choose to give me that money, I'll be able to afford a delicious meal this instant. And because I'm unemployed, I REALLY need this money. Since giving money to those who need it is an act of kindness, why does it matter who it's given to?

t_ch: 懂了吗？「乞讨」是一种高尚的生活方式～我是在满足客户们「乐善好施」的情绪价值。
t_en: You got it now? Begging is a noble way of life! I am fulfilling my clients' emotional value in "performing an act of kindness."

t_ch: 聊聊你在找的那位朋友。
t_en: Let's talk about that friend you're looking for.

t_ch: 嗐，其实我也没有见过他本人，只是星际暗网上认识的「网友」。说实话，我俩也不算熟悉，网友嘛，都是因为共同的爱好聚集一起的。
t_en: *sigh* I've never actually seen him in person, he's just an internet friend I knew on the interstellar dark net. Honestly, we're not even that close. We only connect because of shared interests.

t_ch: 我们之间的共同点就是在于「畅聊毁灭世界的方法」…久而久之，也算是小有名声——包括我在内，有四个人被并称为「四绝」。
t_en: Our common ground lies in "discussing ways to destroy the world"... And since then, we gathered a small following. Including me, there are four of us called the "Four Greats."

t_ch: 咱们这四绝，每人都有自己的特点——东邪、西毒、南帝、北丐。你一听就明白，我就是那个「北丐」，而我等的人是「南帝」。
t_en: We all have our own specialties — Eastern Heretic, Western Venom, Southern Emperor, and Northern Beggar. You'll get it immediately. I'm that "Northern Beggar," and the person I'm waiting for is "Southern Emperor."

t_ch: 其他二位我瞧不上，这南帝可不简单。他很少说话，却有一种不言自明的说服力，难道他真是什么大人物？
t_en: I scoff at the other two, but this Southern Emperor is a mysterious one. He rarely speaks, but has some sort of inexplicable persuasiveness to him. Could he really be some big shot?

t_ch: 南帝说参加完什么盛会就来会会朋友，我相信他。
t_en: The Southern Emperor said he'd meet a friend after attending some banquet. I believe him.

t_ch: （拿出「毁烬焚骨的大公」）
t_en: (Take out The Ashblazing Grand Duke)

t_ch: 说实话，我不太相信这是他的遗物，尽管这真的很像……
t_en: Honestly, I have trouble believing this is what he left behind, even if it looks really authentic...

t_ch: 南帝有可能永远来不了了…
t_en: The Southern Emperor may never be able to come...

t_ch: 出交通事故了吗？哎，真是世事难料啊。
t_en: Could he have gotten into an accident? *sigh* That's how unpredictable life is.

t_ch: 如果他永远地离开了，我愿意朝着熊熊燃烧的火焰向他致以敬意——我无法认可他的理想，但是我赞扬他奔赴理想的姿态，永不屈服，永不妥协，永远纯粹！
t_en: If he's really gone forever, I will face the roiling flames and pay my sincere respects to him. I can't condone his ideals, but I admire the way he pursued them — undaunted, unwavering, and untainted!

t_ch: 有没有什么要饭的经验？
t_en: Do you have any experience in begging?

t_ch: 我爱说实话——要饭这技术活，在乞丐业界，我说是第二，没人敢称第一。
t_en: Honesty's my middle name — when it comes to skills in begging, in this domain, I'm pretty much the de facto leader.

t_ch: 就说这要饭的对象，不能太光鲜，不然他铁定嫌弃你，也不能太穷酸，不然你还得倒贴他几块钱。
t_en: Take targets for begging, for example: They can't look too affluent, or they'll definitely snub you. But they also can't be too impoverished, or you'll end up giving him handouts instead.

t_ch: 至于要饭的吉祥话，就更是难了，见了公司的人说「愿琥珀王保佑你」，见了博识学会的学者要说「愿博识尊保佑你」，啥都分不清的，就帝弓保佑。
t_en: As for begging taglines, that's even harder. If you see an IPC fellow, you have to say, "May the Amber Lord protect you." If it's an Intelligentsia Guild scholar, it's "May Nous guide you." If you can't really tell, the Reignbow will bless them.

t_ch: 另外就是要知足，不能嫌弃多少。要是拿太多了，你要分给其他人；要是拿太少了，你也不能有怨气…毕竟没有别人的慷慨，你啥也不是！
t_en: Also, contentment is key — you can't be picky about the offering. If it's too much, you distribute some of it to others. If it's too little, you can't gripe about it either... I mean, you're nothing without the benevolence of others!

t_ch: 今天就先聊到这里吧。
t_en: We'll catch up another time.

t_ch: 愿帝弓保佑你！
t_en: May the Reignbow bless you!

t_ch: 今天出门没有带钱，下次吧。
t_en: I haven't got cash on me today, another time.

t_ch: 没事儿，下次我也在这儿等您——愿帝弓保佑你！
t_en: All good, I'll be waiting here for you. May the Reignbow bless you!

t_ch: 给你钱，你给我来点情绪价值。
t_en: Here's some money, lay some emotional value on me.

t_ch: 愿帝弓保佑你！
t_en: May the Reignbow bless you!

s_ch: 即将出差的天舶司飞行士
t_ch: 这次司部的差事要去的是个丛林世界，啧，那儿会不会虫子特别多？
t_en: This time around we're going to a jungle world. Tsk, do you think there'll be loads of insects there?

s_ch: 告别朋友的天舶司文员
s_en: Sky-Faring Commission Staff Bidding Goodbye to Friend
t_ch: 别嫌了，一般仙舟人哪儿能像你这样跑东跑西，好羡慕啊。
t_en: Don't worry about it. Nobody on Xianzhou gets to travel all over the place like you do. I'm jealous.

s_ch: 告别朋友的天舶司文员
s_en: Sky-Faring Commission Staff Bidding Goodbye to Friend
t_ch: 一路保重，注意安全。
t_en: Take care and stay safe along the way.

s_ch: 即将出差的天舶司飞行士
t_ch: 放心吧，我会带特产回来的！
t_en: Don't worry. I'll bring back some local specialties!

s_ch: 告别朋友的天舶司文员
s_en: Sky-Faring Commission Staff Bidding Goodbye to Friend
t_ch: 那里有什么特产？
t_en: What do they have there?

s_ch: 即将出差的天舶司飞行士
t_ch: 嗯…鼻行兽算不算？
t_en: Um... does a Nosewalker count?

s_ch: 告别朋友的天舶司文员
s_en: Sky-Faring Commission Staff Bidding Goodbye to Friend
t_ch: 酷耶，早就想养一只！
t_en: Cool, I've been wanting one of those for ages!

s_ch: 即将出差的天舶司飞行士
t_ch: 再搞一只学舌妖回来好了…啊，恐怕会有生态问题。
t_en: Bring back a Parrot Goblin too... Ah, but there'll probably cause an issue with the ecosystem.

t_ch: 好厉害啊，卷起来了
t_en: Simply amazing, it's rolled up.

t_ch: 卷起来有什么厉害的。
t_en: What's so amazing about rolling something up?

t_ch: 那你会卷吗？
t_en: Well, can you do it?

t_ch: 我又不是卷轴我不卷。
t_en: I'm not a scroll, so I can't be rolled up.

t_ch: 织机好大！
t_en: What a massive loom!

t_ch: 小心，往前会被卷进去哦。
t_en: Careful, or you'll get rolled up inside.

t_ch: 那就变成了二次元生物了！
t_en: I don't wanna become a 2D creature!

t_ch: 这么高，掉下去怎么办？
t_en: It's so high. What am I gonna do if I fall?

t_ch: 亲爱的，我问过仙舟人啦！
t_en: Darling, I asked the Xianzhou people!

t_ch: 掉下去的人不会受伤。
t_en: Falling down doesn't lead to any injuries.

t_ch: 掉到洞天视界边缘…
t_en: If you fall down to the edge of the delve...

t_ch: 会无限处于静滞状态。
t_en: You'll be placed in stasis indefinitely.

t_ch: 你的心里只有科学！
t_en: All you care about is your science!

t_ch: 根本没有我！
t_en: Not me!

t_ch: 这次张贴广告是你负责的？
t_en: Are you responsible for the poster this time?

t_ch: 没错啊，你看这互补色多好看。
t_en: Yup, just look how beautiful these complimentary colors are.

t_ch: 其实颜色可以再素一些。
t_en: Actually, I think they could be a little less garish.

t_ch: 甲方都满意了，你算老几？
t_en: The outsourcer is satisfied with the colors. Who are you to talk?

t_ch: 您好，请问有推荐的茶饮吗？
t_en: Hello, do you have any recommended teas today?

t_ch: 当季首选鳞渊春。
t_en: Nothing beats Whale-Tide Spring this time of year.

t_ch: 喝茶其实有很多讲究的…
t_en: There're actually lots of details to pay attention to when sampling tea...

t_ch: 请给我来一杯可乐。
t_en: Please can I have a glass of cola?

t_ch: 买这些首饰的时候…
t_en: When you bought these pieces of jewelry...

t_ch: 花了<unbreak>27</unbreak>万。
t_en: They cost <unbreak>270</unbreak> grand.

t_ch: 去典当铺鉴定后…
t_en: When you went for an appraisal at the pawn shop...

t_ch: 就值<unbreak>27</unbreak>镝。
t_en: They were worth just <unbreak>27</unbreak> strales.

t_ch: 我们不会是上当了吧？
t_en: Did we get scammed?

t_ch: 你是说当时还是现在？
t_en: Do you mean then, or now?

t_ch: 你听说了吗？那个！
t_en: Did you hear the news? You know!

t_ch: 啊！你说那个，我听说了。
t_en: Oh yeah, that old chestnut. Yup, I heard.

t_ch: 那也太那个了吧…
t_en: Oh em gee, it's so like, y'know...

t_ch: 哎，我也没想到。
t_en: Yeah, who would've thought?

t_ch: 什么加密通话？
t_en: Is this a secret code or something?

t_ch: 我打算辞去天舶司的工作。
t_en: I plan on resigning from the Sky-Faring Commission.

t_ch: 辞职之后打算做些什么？
t_en: What'll you do once you've resigned?

t_ch: 计程槎飞行士就挺不错的。
t_en: Starskiff piloting sounds good to me.

t_ch: 好啊，到时我给你开罚单。
t_en: Cool, then I can give you speeding tickets.

t_ch: 虽然你说你喜欢我。
t_en: I know you said you liked me.

t_ch: 可是我不喜欢小屁孩儿。
t_en: But I just don't like kids.

t_ch: 可、可你也是小屁孩儿…
t_en: B—But you're just a kid yourself...

t_ch: 引擎输出功率平稳，但是转速有些不太自然的浮动，到底是什么原因呢……
t_en: The engine's power output is stable, but the revolution rate is fluctuating unnaturally. What exactly is the reason...

t_ch: 咦，有事找我吗…啊，不对，你是谁？这里是引擎舱，你为什么会出现在这里？
t_en: Oh, you're looking for me...? Ah, wait, who are you again? This is the engine room. Why are you here?

t_ch: 不好意思，我迷路了。
t_en: Sorry, I got lost.

t_ch: 观众席和引擎舱之间隔了好几层甲板，你不会是开着导航迷路的吧？
t_en: The spectator floor and engine room are separated by several levels. You got lost with your Navigation on?

t_ch: 其实我是引擎工程师。
t_en: I'm the engine engineer.

t_ch: 我负责左引擎，你负责右引擎是吧？
t_en: I'm in charge of the left engine, so that means you're responsible for the right one, right?

t_ch: 不许乱动，我要劫船！
t_en: No one move, I'm skyjacking this ship!

t_ch: 呃…容许我善意地提醒你一下，劫船一般是劫驾驶舱，你要不换个地方？
t_en: Err... Might I kindly remind you that skyjackers usually take the cockpit? Consider recalibrating your Navigation device?

t_ch: 等等！你这张脸我好像在哪里见过！竞锋舰的捕狼行动前，好像同步过信息…你不会就是那位星穹列车的无名客{NICKNAME}吧？！
t_en: Hang on! I've seen your face somewhere! Before the Skysplitter's Wolfcatcher Operation, I think we've synced up intel... You're not {NICKNAME}, the Nameless of the Astral Express, are you!?

t_ch: 刚才真是失礼了。我脑子被工作塞满了，一下子没认出您…不过您就算是罗浮贵客，这引擎舱终归是竞锋舰的核心重地，希望你不要随意操作这里的任何东西。
t_en: I apologize for my rudeness. My brain's been overloaded by work, and I didn't recognize you immediately... But even if you're a Luofu VIP, the engine room is crucial to the Skysplitter — I really hope you don't touch anything in here.

t_ch: 不会，我动这引擎干什么…
t_en: Course not, why would I touch this engine...

t_ch: 咱是同步过信息的，我听说您比较擅长开玩笑，既然你说不会，我就放心了。
t_en: We've synced up before, and I hear that you're quite the joker. But if you say you won't, I'll take your word for it.

t_ch: 你说不行，就真的不行吗？
t_en: When you say, "Don't touch anything," did you really mean it?

t_ch: 怎么还要质疑我呢…不行就是不行。等等，你不会真的要试试吧，我劝您不要！
t_en: I can't believe you're doubting me... No means no. Hold it, you can't be serious, are you? I'm sincerely asking you not to!

t_ch: 你放心好了，你看不见的。
t_en: You don't have to worry one bit, you won't see a thing.

t_ch: 那我放心了……
t_en: Phew, that's good then...

t_ch: …个鬼啊！作为一名引擎工程师，我非常理解您的好奇心。但是求您了，千万别让我们为难。
t_en: ...Not! Look, I'm an engine engineer, I totally understand your curiosity. But please, don't make this difficult for us.

t_ch: …不过话又说回来，若不是您是星穹列车的无名客，一想到列车引擎还完好无损，我对您还是充满了信任的。
t_en: ...But on the other hand, you're a Nameless on the Astral Express. The fact that the train's engine is still in pristine condition means I can completely trust you.

t_ch: 若是您实在对引擎舱很感兴趣，我可以在不涉密的前提下，为您简单科普一下。
t_en: If you're truly curious about the engine room, I can give you a brief overview without revealing any technical secrets.

t_ch: 我们又见面了，这里看上去也没什么好逛的，您若是对引擎感兴趣，可以直接问我。
t_en: We meet again. There's nothing else interesting to look at here. If you're curious about the engine, you can ask me anything.

t_ch: 那我们随便聊聊吧…
t_en: Let's banter then...

t_ch: 只要不涉及敏感问题，我都会尽力解答。
t_en: As long as it doesn't involve sensitive questions, I'll answer the best I can.

t_ch: 我想了解下竞锋舰引擎。
t_en: I'd like to know more about the Skysplitter's engine.

t_ch: 太好了，自从成为星舰工程师之后，我的人生梦想第二名就是向别人解释引擎的工作原理——
t_en: Wonderful! Since I became an interstellar ship engineer, the second item on my lifelong wish list has been to regale another person with the fundamentals about engine functionality...

t_ch: 它涉及到引擎的运行原理、机械结构、材料特性、生产工艺…但是，这些我都不能告诉您，请您不要问这样可能涉密的问题。
t_en: It involves the operating principles of engines, mechanical structures, material properties, and the artisanship of the engine's production... However, I can't disclose any of these to you. Please refrain from asking such potentially sensitive questions.

t_ch: 你只需要知道的是，这对庞然大物产生的动力足以推动一颗小行星。
t_en: All you need to know is that the power generated by this colossal entity can move a small asteroid.

t_ch: 怎么样，是不是很酷？
t_en: How about that! Isn't it cool?

t_ch: 引擎工程师具体做些什么？
t_en: What do engine engineers exactly do?

t_ch: 简而言之，就是确保引擎维持正常的运行状态。一旦有任何指标出现异常，我都要及时定位问题，排除故障。
t_en: In simple terms, it's about ensuring the engines maintain normal functionality. If any indicators show an abnormality, I need to promptly identify and rectify the issue.

t_ch: 引擎对于竞锋舰，就像心脏对于人是一样的。但凡它出一点问题…最直接的结果就是大家一起手拉手告别余生。
t_en: For the Skysplitter, the engine is akin to a heart in the human body. Any malfunction... and the most immediate result is everyone stepping into the afterlife, hand-in-hand.

t_ch: 所以这份工作最重要的就是责任心和细心，不能放过一点微小的隐患。魔鬼往往隐藏在细节中，我需要把它们一点点揪出来。
t_en: So, the most crucial aspects of this job are responsibility and meticulousness, leaving no room for oversight. Often, the devil is in the details, and I must root them out one by one.

t_ch: 有空的话上去看看比赛吧。
t_en: If you're free, go up and watch the competition.

t_ch: 可惜，比赛期间我一般都会在这里随时待命…如果引擎出了问题找不到人就危险了，我的假期基本在竞锋舰没有比赛的时候。
t_en: Unfortunately, during competition season, I'm usually on standby here... If something goes wrong with the engine and there's no one around, it could be dangerous. My vacations are mostly when there aren't tournaments happening on the Skysplitter.

t_ch: 刚得知要来竞锋舰工作的时候，我还有些窃喜。没想到自从我进了引擎舱就再也没出去过，即使赛场就在头顶不远处，也像是隔着银河那么远。
t_en: When I first learned that I'd be working on the Skysplitter, I was somewhat excited. Who knew that once I stepped into the engine room, I'd never be able to leave. Even though the arena is just above, it feels like a galaxy away.

t_ch: 不过还好有赛事解说的广播，有震天动地的欢呼声，算是我独有的临场感。
t_en: Luckily, there's still a broadcast of the competition commentary and earth-shaking cheers — like my very own VIP seat in the heart of the action.

t_ch: 有机会的话，得请你看场比赛。
t_en: If there's a chance, I'll invite you to watch a match.

t_ch: 如果有机会的话，哈哈，谢谢您。
t_en: If there's a chance, haha, thank you.

t_ch: 比赛的成功，肯定少不了你啊…
t_en: The success of the competition wouldn't be possible without you...

t_ch: 一直以来这事咱们就自己心里知道，谢谢你，愿意来了解我们的工作。
t_en: That's something we all know, but don't mention. Thank you for taking the time to understand our work.

t_ch: 要是有才华横溢的工程师来跟我换班就好了，哈哈，现在我还站好这班岗吧。
t_en: If only there's a talented engineer who can swap shifts with me, that would be great. Haha, for now I'll just hold down the fort!

t_ch: 我想问的差不多了。
t_en: That's all I wanted to ask.

t_ch: 好的，祝您在竞锋舰一切顺遂！那句话怎么说的，啊，「祝你宝剑常锋」！
t_en: Okay, please have a smooth voyage aboard the Skysplitter! How does that line go... Ah, may your sword be ever-sharp!

t_ch: 我差不多该离开了。
t_en: I should leave soon.

t_ch: 好的，祝您在竞锋舰一切顺遂！那句话怎么说的，啊，「祝你宝剑常锋」！
t_en: Okay, please have a smooth voyage aboard the Skysplitter! How does that line go... Ah, may your sword be ever-sharp!

t_ch: 好将军！寿瘟犯境动刀兵～
t_en: O General! The plague approaches; tell troops to listen~

t_ch: 我与我娘亲逃外星～
t_en: My mom and I escaped to an off-world system~

t_ch: 颠沛流离走失了母亲～
t_en: Cast adrift, I lost my mom~

t_ch: 怎不叫人好伤心！
t_en: I just can't bear to think of losing my mom!

t_ch: 唱得好啊！
t_en: You're a really great singer!

t_ch: 请问您需要怎样托运服务？
t_en: Excuse me, what kind of consignment services are you after?

t_ch: 我有两箱药材要从仙舟运出。
t_en: I have two boxes of meds to be shipped out of Xianzhou.

t_ch: 我们需要确认天舶司文件。
t_en: We need to verify your Sky-Faring Commission documentation.

t_ch: 这事儿不能让天舶司知道。
t_en: You can't let the Sky-Faring Commission know about this.

t_ch: 先生，您还是换一家托运吧。
t_en: Sir, you should choose another carrier.

t_ch: 把你这货箱赶紧开走！
t_en: Get your cargo crate outta here!

t_ch: 凭什么呀……
t_en: Based on what...?

t_ch: 你再不开走我就贴条了！
t_en: If you don't get lost, I'll give you a ticket!

t_ch: 我真的不会开……
t_en: Honestly, I can't even drive...

t_ch: 我贴好了，你去交罚款吧！
t_en: You've been ticketed. Go pay your fine!

t_ch: 我不知道这是谁的货箱。
t_en: I don't know whose crate this is.

t_ch: 仙人快乐茶推出新品了。
t_en: Immortal's Delight has launched a new tea.

t_ch: 少喝点儿甜水，全是糖。
t_en: You should drink less tea, as it's so sugary.

t_ch: 现在有无糖的配方了。
t_en: Their new recipe is sugar-free though.

t_ch: 那都是骗小孩儿的。
t_en: That's just how they trick kids.

t_ch: 你不是买了箱无糖汽水？
t_en: Didn't you buy a whole case of sugar-free soda?

t_ch: 成年人的生活需要欺骗。
t_en: Trickery is encouraged when you're an adult.

t_ch: 这台售货机再也不说话了…
t_en: This vending machine doesn't speak anymore...

t_ch: 它帮助了我，让我走出低谷。
t_en: It helped me out before when I was at rock bottom.

t_ch: 好想再和它聊聊天……
t_en: I really want to chat with it again...

t_ch: 再见了，我会好好生活的。
t_en: Farewell. I'm going to live out my best life.

t_ch: 来了您呐，这边儿请！
t_en: Welcome. This way, please!

t_ch: 这边儿有座儿，这边儿宽敞。
t_en: Here's a seat. It's quite spacious.

t_ch: 把你的东西收拾收拾。
t_en: Pack up your things.

t_ch: 这里经过多不方便啊。
t_en: It's such a pain going through here.

t_ch: 知道了，知道了。
t_en: I know, I know.

t_ch: 我又不是在占道经营。
t_en: I'm not a street hawker.

t_ch: 我兄弟在星际和平公司…
t_en: My brother works at the IPC...

t_ch: 一天工作四十二小时！
t_en: He works <unbreak>42</unbreak> hours a day!

t_ch: 一天有那么多时间吗？
t_en: Are there really that many hours in a day?

t_ch: 那个星球自转周期长
t_en: It's a planet with a slow rotational period.

t_ch: 我兄弟在公司的工业世界干活，一天要工作四十二个小时。
t_en: My bro works <unbreak>42</unbreak>-hour days at the industrial zone.

t_ch: 吓死人了！等等，一天有那么多时间吗？
t_en: That's too much! Hang on, are there really that many hours in the day?

t_ch: 那个星球自转周期比较长。
t_en: It's a planet with a slow rotational period.

t_ch: 那个星球一天一共多长？
t_en: How many hours are there in a day there?

t_ch: 四十八个小时。
t_en: <unbreak>48</unbreak> hours.

t_ch: 还是很可怕啊！让不让人活了？
t_en: That's still too much! Can't they let people live a little?

t_ch: 唉，不工作才活不下去啊。只有反物质军团去过的世界，人们才不用工作。
t_en: Ah, you can't live without work. The only worlds on which you don't have to work are the ones that've been "visited" by the Antimatter Legion.

t_ch: 还、还有不用工作的世界？
t_en: S—So they don't even have to work?

t_ch: 人都被扬成灰了，还工作个啥？
t_en: They can't. They're all ashes.

t_ch: 这儿便是天舶司的办公地…
t_en: This is the Sky-Faring Commission's office.

t_ch: 名唤司辰宫。
t_en: People know it as the Palace of Astrum.

t_ch: 噢，形制还蛮古雅的。
t_en: Oh! The architecture has an air of classic elegance to it.

t_ch: 我们那边这样风格的建筑越来越少了
t_en: At where we live, we don't see many buildings erected in such a style anymore.

t_ch: 我不是来观赏演武仪典的…
t_en: I'm not here for the Wardance...

t_ch: 那您来这儿干嘛？
t_en: Then what are you doing here?

t_ch: 完成学会的论文…
t_en: I'm here to complete my thesis for the Guild...

t_ch: 论文？
t_en: Thesis?

t_ch: 《论碳基生命的仪式性战斗》…
t_en: Ritualistic Battles of Carbon-Based Lifeforms: A Study...

t_ch: 咳咳…来科学考察的吗？
t_en: ...Ahem, I see. Are you here on a scientific expedition?

t_ch: 基于公司与仙舟的深度合作…
t_en: The IPC and the Xianzhou have been working closely together...

t_ch: 我们赞助了本次演武仪典。
t_en: So we're sponsoring the Wardance this time.

t_ch: 可惜驭空司舵不给冠名权…
t_en: It's a shame that Helm Master Yukong refused to give us naming rights...

t_ch: …给公司冠名？
t_en: ...You mean to the IPC?

t_ch: 那不就成了公司的演武仪典吗？
t_en: Wouldn't that make the Wardance the IPC's?

t_ch: 但在其他世界…
t_en: But in other worlds...

t_ch: 一般咱们都能得到冠名权啊？
t_en: We're usually able to obtain naming rights.

t_ch: 我听说书都听得热血沸腾了…
t_en: I'm so pumped up after hearing the storytellers' tales...

t_ch: 所以真的可以御剑飞行吗？
t_en: Is it really possible to fly on a sword?

t_ch: 可以，得练上个几十年吧…
t_en: Yes, but it requires decades of training...

t_ch: 那太久了…
t_en: That's too long...

t_ch: 演武仪典什么时候召开啊？
t_en: When will the Wardance be held?

t_ch: 等「竞锋舰」开放登舰…
t_en: When the Skysplitter is open for boarding...

t_ch: 那竞锋舰什么时候开放登舰？
t_en: Then when will the Skysplitter be open for boarding?

t_ch: 等演武仪典召开…
t_en: When they hold the Wardance...

t_ch: 说了等于没说啊。
t_en: That's not helping.

t_ch: 就是就是，给透点消息呗。
t_en: Yeah! Can't you tell us just a bit of information?

t_ch: 来罗浮的游客变多了…
t_en: We're getting more and more tourists coming to the Luofu...

t_ch: 演武仪典听说邀请了不少外宾…
t_en: I heard that they invited a good number of outworlder guests to the Wardance...

t_ch: 将军为此准备了很久啊
t_en: The general is putting in a lot of time into the preparations.

t_ch: 整个天舶司都连轴转了
t_en: The entire Sky-Faring Commission has been working round the clock.

t_ch: 地衡司这边也没差。
t_en: It's the same here in the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 希望来的游客都能满意吧…
t_en: Ah, I hope the tourists find it to their liking...

t_ch: 我爸爸已经订好了票…
t_en: Daddy has already reserved tickets!

t_ch: 我都等不及咧！
t_en: I can't wait!

t_ch: 帮我向彦卿大人要个签名吧…
t_en: Can you help me get Master Yanqing's autograph?

t_ch: 签什么呢？
t_en: What do you want him to write as a message?

t_ch: 加强自己！
t_en: "Work hard to improve yourself"!

t_ch: 听说其他将军也会来…
t_en: I heard that other generals will also be there...

t_ch: 我早知道了…
t_en: I already knew that...

t_ch: 曜青和朱明的人陆续到了不少。
t_en: Many from the Yaoqing and the Zhuming have also come.

t_ch: 你没事吧？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 人没事…脑袋有事。
t_en: By and large, yes. My head, on the other hand...

t_ch: 我们也算是协同作战了…
t_en: I suppose we're now comrades who have fought alongside each other...

t_ch: 毕竟脑袋也是武器的一种…
t_en: After all, the head can also be used for offensive maneuvers...

t_ch: 我的天…那就是步离人？
t_en: Oh my! Is that a borisin?

t_ch: 拍了不少素材…传到网上吧
t_en: I've taken quite a few photos... Time to upload them.

t_ch: 起个标题吧，就叫…
t_en: I've already thought of the caption...

t_ch: 《仙舟动物权益保护状况日益堪忧》
t_en: "Animal rights protection are an increasingly concerning issue in Xianzhou."

t_ch: 朋友，不要走！你…不会是想去看那些名门正道的剑斗吧？
t_en: Don't go, my friend! Are you... going to watch those prestigious sword duels?

t_ch: 不看名门正道我看什么？
t_en: What should I be watching, if not prestigious sword duels?

t_ch: 你说的有道理。名门正派确实很适合表演，却不适合你。
t_en: You're right. The orthodox sects offer suitable performances, but they are not suited to your tastes.

t_ch: 我对仙舟功夫兴趣寥寥…
t_en: I have little interest in the martial arts of Xianzhou...

t_ch: 没事，咱这也不算是「正统仙舟功夫」，也许你会感兴趣。
t_en: No worries, this doesn't really count as "traditional Xianzhou martial arts," maybe you'll be interested.

t_ch: 既然话都说到这里了，我也不再遮遮掩掩了。你看我体格瘦弱，实不相瞒，我从小就受到了其他小孩的欺辱，于是那个时候的我想要投身武术。
t_en: Since I've already said so much, I won't beat around the bush. You see, I'm kinda scrawny. Honestly, I've been bullied since childhood. Since then, I've been wanting to get into martial arts.

t_ch: 可是，就在入学不久后我得知了一个消息——负责授课的武术师范在学校外面被星际海盗暴打，从那时起，我便决心背弃「好讲武德」的传统仙舟功夫。
t_en: However, shortly after enrolling, I received news that our martial arts instructor was beaten up by interstellar pirates outside the school. From that moment on, I've been determined to abandon the traditional and honorable Xianzhou martial arts.

t_ch: 所以，我希望你能加入我们无限制格斗门。一把剪刀，一块板砖，也能把恶棍打得落花流水！不要让正义，被困在懦弱的身体！
t_en: So, I hope you can join our Lawless Brawl school. With a pair of scissors or a brick, you can make villains fall like leaves in the wind! Don't let justice be trapped in a weak body!

t_ch: 所以，你都教什么？
t_en: Er, so what are you teaching?

t_ch: 背后偷袭，欺诈暗算，群而攻之，用各种随手可得的器械，用你能想象到的所有街头混混打架的招式、野路子，用最系统性的方法进行总结。
t_en: Backstabbing, underhanded tactics, ganging up on opponents, weaponizing anything you pick up, any sort of street brawl tactics and unorthodox methods you can think of in the most systematic manner.

t_ch: 不择手段、不计后果地打倒敌人，总而言之，我会教你怎么赢。
t_en: Using any means necessary, without considering any consequences to bring down the enemy. In other words, I'll teach you how to win.

t_ch: 你看我需要你的格斗技吗？
t_en: Do you need to see my skills?

t_ch: 看样子你是一名自以为是的强者。我告诉你，我曾经战胜过无数如此自傲的人。
t_en: You look like quite the arrogant powerhouse. Just so you know, I've taken down plenty like you before.

t_ch: 所以你要挑战击败幻胧的我吗？
t_en: So you wish to challenge me, the one who defeated Phantylia?

t_ch: 幻、幻胧？
t_en: Ph—Phantylia?

t_ch: 所以我们要不上擂台试试高下？
t_en: Should we step into the arena and see who comes out on top?

t_ch: 对不起，我不接受任何性质的比武。我的无限制格斗门只会用于暴徒、歹徒，绝对不会对武林中人出手。
t_en: Sorry, I don't engage in any form of competitive combat. My Lawless Brawler arts are only for thugs and villains, never for martial artists.

t_ch: 你这些功夫是合法的吗？
t_en: Is your type of martial art even legal?

t_ch: 很多人问过我相同的问题，我开馆授徒的第一课就是讲仙舟法律和无限自卫权。
t_en: Many people have asked me the same question. The first lesson I teach at my school is about the laws of Xianzhou and unlimited self-defense.

t_ch: 我曾经有个弟子被<unbreak>12</unbreak>名星际海盗围攻，用了我的「无限制格斗」的技法，不仅毫发无损，还当场击毙一名海盗，最后被定性为正当防卫。
t_en: One of my disciples was once surrounded by twelve interstellar pirates. By using my "Lawless Brawler Style" technique, not only did he emerge unscathed, but he also fatally wounded a pirate on the spot. Eventually, it was ruled as justifiable self-defense.

t_ch: 如今我开门立派也算是有些时日了，正需要一些你这样的弟子打出名声，你有没有兴趣？
t_en: I've been running my school for a while now. I'm in need of disciples like you to make a name for my school. Are you interested?

t_ch: 如此不择手段的攻击，啧啧称奇！如此毫无限制的打斗，厉害异常！如此新型独特的器械，令人叹服！宁当疯狗，不当懦夫，希望你也能掌握无限制格斗术！
t_en: Such underhanded attacks, truly astounding! Such unbridled brawling technique, extraordinarily formidable! And what unique, cutting-edge equipment you possess! Resignedly impressive! One would rather be a rabid hound than a coward, I hope you, too, will master the art of the Lawless Brawler!

t_ch: 怎么样，今天是否有兴趣了解一下？
t_en: How about it, are you interested in learning more today?

t_ch: 打算深入了解一下。
t_en: Decide to gain a more in-depth understanding.

t_ch: 你要是能感兴趣，那是最好不过了，想知道什么都可以问，我鹤高必倾囊相授。
t_en: It'd be fantastic if you're really interested. If there's anything you'd like to know, ask away! I'm happy to impart my knowledge generously.

t_ch: 无限制格斗流是什么？
t_en: What is the "Lawless Brawler Style"?

t_ch: 我明白，你可能还对无限制格斗没有什么认知，毕竟很少有人愿意发自内心承认我的武学造诣为一种「功夫流派」，说实话，我根本不在乎。
t_en: I understand, you might not be familiar with "Lawless Brawl." After all, not many are willing to acknowledge my martial arts as a form of "style." Honestly, I don't really care.

t_ch: 要问咱门派最经典的招式，必然是疯狗拳和爆丸腿。从名字里你也能听出，这两招，强调的就是疯和爆。
t_en: If you're asking about our most classic moves, it's definitely the Crazy Dog Fist and Explosive Marble Kick. From just the names, you can tell that these two moves emphasize craziness and explosiveness.

t_ch: 疯，就是让肢体变得凌乱，让敌人抓不住你的动作。爆，就是招招致命，一定往最痛最下三滥的弱点狠狠攻击。
t_en: "Crazy" means making your moves chaotic, so enemies can't predict your next move. "Explosive" means every move is deadly, aiming straight for the most painful and despicable weak points.

t_ch: 只要能融会贯通这些技巧，管你是戳眼睛，吐口水还是踩脚趾，都能举一反三，事半功倍。
t_en: Once you master these techniques, whether it's eye poking, spitting or toe stomping — they can be applied universally and double your efficiency.

t_ch: 当然，能举报绝不打架，能群殴绝不单挑，能持械绝不空手。稳，才是无限制格斗的核心。
t_en: Of course, if reporting is an option, avoid fighting, if ganging up is possible, don't go solo, and if you can wield weapons, never go empty-handed. Stability is the core of Lawless Brawl Style.

t_ch: 感觉有些残暴。
t_en: Feels a little inhumane.

t_ch: 别担心，我不会对普通人使用如此恶毒的招式。
t_en: Don't worry, I won't use such vicious techniques against normal people.

t_ch: 似乎不符合法律…
t_en: It seems a little illegal...

t_ch: 别担心，我禁止法制观念浅薄者学习。
t_en: Don't worry, I forbid those who have a pale understanding of the law from learning.

t_ch: 小孩子能看？
t_en: Is this child-friendly?

t_ch: 别担心，我拒绝未成年者学习。
t_en: Don't worry, I forbid underage kids from learning my art.

t_ch: 不必担心，我们门派的学员，在出师前都会熟读仙舟刑法，保证行为不会超出正当防卫的范畴——如果徒弟出事了坐牢，就不能说是我的徒弟！
t_en: Don't worry, the students of my school will thoroughly study Xianzhou's criminal law before graduating. They will ensure that their actions stay within the boundaries of justifiable self-defense. If a disciple gets into trouble and ends up in jail, they are not considered my disciple!

t_ch: 感觉你会招惹同行…
t_en: Feels like you might be provoking others who share a trade as you...

t_ch: 他们那群人啊，恨我恨得是牙痒痒。还说什么，「无限制格斗不讲武德，一点都不雅观，是在丢咱们仙舟武学的脸！」
t_en: Those folks really hate me to the core. They even say, "Lawless Brawl" has no honor, it's undignified and embarrassing our Xianzhou martial arts!

t_ch: 嘿！我在乎吗？再好看的招式，打不过就是花拳绣腿，劫匪可不会因为你的招式漂亮就乖乖自首。
t_en: Hmph! Do I look like I care? No matter how flashy your moves are, if you don't win, you're just punching air! Bandits won't just surrender because your moves look cool.

t_ch: 你到长乐天的各大拳馆打听打听，骂得声量最大的，哪个敢和我放开了打？
t_en: Go ask around the major boxing gyms at the Exalting Sanctum. Those who trash-talk the loudest, who dares to spar with me openly?

t_ch: 就算我什么也不做，就是站在面前，他们的膝盖和屁股也要隐隐幻痛呢。
t_en: Even if I do nothing but stand before them, they'll feel a twinge in their knees and behinds.

t_ch: 你是来竞锋舰上比武的吗？
t_en: Are you competing on the Skysplitter?

t_ch: 说实话，我不觉得自己有资格在竞锋舰比赛，再说了，我的所有武学都是为了对付暴徒的，而不是那些武林中人，那些真正的高手。
t_en: To be frank, I don't think I'm qualified to compete on the Skysplitter. Besides, all my martial arts are angled towards dealing with thugs and not those martial arts experts. They are the real masters.

t_ch: 但我想要大家能看到无限制格斗，有更多人对我的无限制格斗感兴趣就行了。
t_en: But I hope more people will see the Lawless Brawl and gain more interest in it.

t_ch: 不是所有人都能像彦卿骁卫和将军那样强。我们平凡人只用比坏人厉害一点，比过去勇敢一点，就足以成为自己的英雄。
t_en: Not everyone can be as strong as Lieutenant Yanqing and the General. But if we ordinary folks can just be a bit tougher than the thugs and a bit braver than yesterday, we can be the heroes in our own stories.

t_ch: 无限制格斗，就是属于我们普通人的武器。
t_en: The Lawless Brawl is a weapon that belongs to us ordinary people.

t_ch: 先聊到这儿吧。
t_en: Good chatting with you.

t_ch: 没关系，朋友，无限制格斗的大门永远会为你敞开。
t_en: Don't worry, my friend. The doors of the Lawless Brawl are always open to you.

t_ch: 暂时还不感兴趣。
t_en: Currently uninterested.

t_ch: 没关系，朋友，无限制格斗的大门永远会为你敞开。
t_en: Don't worry, my friend. The doors of the Lawless Brawl are always open to you.

t_ch: 镇妖塔轻轻浮起，你想要与哪位岁阳对话？
t_en: The suppression towers float gently. Which heliobus would you like to talk to?

t_ch: 阿灼。
t_en: Luxie.

t_ch: 大人还真是麻烦，已经抓住我了，还有这么多的事……
t_en: Grown ups are such a pain. You've already caught me. What more could you want...

t_ch: 想了解阿灼的故事…
t_en: I'd like to hear Luxie's story...

t_ch: 大人应该给小孩讲故事，没想到这也能颠倒…行吧，我给你讲一个。
t_en: It should be adults telling stories to children, not the other way around... Fine, here's one.

t_ch: 我代表十王司来审问你…
t_en: I am here on behalf of the Ten-Lords Commission to interrogate you...

t_ch: 审什么审，我不接受审判，这样吧，我给你讲个故事呗。
t_en: Interrogate? You wish. I'm not standing trial. Here, let me tell you a story.

t_ch: 之前，我曾经也附身在一个成年人身上，那是一个孩子的父亲。那个小孩着实讨厌，大喊大叫、缺乏教养、还会偷邻居家的食物。
t_en: Once, I attached myself to a father. His kid was so annoying — shouting, yelling, being a brat. Plus, he'd just take food from the neighbors.

t_ch: 那时我感受到了父亲的愤怒，心想着要给那个小孩一些惩罚，我便迷了小孩的眼睛，让他以为自己走进了一座大的空房间里，脚下全是一片炭火。
t_en: Back then, I felt the father's rage and wanted to punish the kid, so I hexed the kid's vision and made him think that he'd walked into a huge room filled with burning coal.

t_ch: 那小孩边哭边跑，跑到门边，却只发现所谓的门根本就不存在，乐得我都不行了。要不是有旁人来叫唤他，这小孩还会继续徒劳地跑下去。
t_en: That kid ran off crying, straight to the door — only to discover that it doesn't exist. It was so fun, I almost couldn't take it. If it weren't for someone calling him, this kid would have kept on running.

t_ch: 然后，你猜怎么着，那小孩双脚就像是烧焦了一般疼痛，但最后也没有吸取教训。
t_en: And guess what happened next? The kid's feet were in excruciating pain, as if they had been burned, but in the end, he still didn't learn his lesson.

t_ch: 你都做了什么呀！
t_en: What did you do!

t_ch: 你放心，没有人在我的幻境里受到真正的伤害。
t_en: Don't worry. Nobody actually got hurt in my little fantasia.

t_ch: 为什么呢，他不怕疼吗？
t_en: Why then? Does he not fear pain?

t_ch: 我窥察到这个小孩无非是想引起父母的注意，但孩子父亲却只是将他看作是负担。小孩不再闹腾了之后，他便满足了「欲望」。
t_en: What I observed was just a child wanting attention from his parents, but the father just saw him as a burden. After the kid stopped making noise, his "desire" had been satisfied.

t_ch: 大人只想着要给个教训，却忘记了所谓「挫折教育」中最重要的部分是教育。
t_en: Adults only think about dishing out lessons, but forget that the most important lesson of all is the education part.

t_ch: 我被恶心到了，决心从他身上脱离。结果大街上，里里外外，到处都有着相似的气味…显然，他们还没有准备好成为父母。
t_en: I was disgusted and decided to get out of his body. But there it was, all over the place. Everywhere I went had the same stench... It seemed like lots of people weren't ready to become parents.

t_ch: 在那一天起，我决心还是附身在小孩身上，给这些大人们来点教训。
t_en: From that day on, I decided to only possess kids so I could teach these adults a lesson.

t_ch: …好了好了，你听得也差不多了，别来烦我了。拜拜！
t_en: ...That's about it, you've pretty much heard it all. Leave me alone. See ya!

t_ch: 明桴。
t_en: Regratta.

t_ch: …有关那艘船的记忆得到了满足，已经航行离开了。你可以称呼我为明桴。
t_en: ...That ship's memory was satisfied, and it already embarked on its next voyage. You may call me Regratta.

t_ch: 一艘船的记忆，有些难以理解。
t_en: A ship's... memory? That's hard to fathom.

t_ch: 通常来说，这确实很难理解。一定是星槎的主人为这艘船投入了太多的感情。
t_en: Generally speaking, this is very hard to fathom. The owner of this starskiff must have invested so much into this ship.

t_ch: 你看上去比别的岁阳更好说话…
t_en: You seem more easy-going than most heliobi...

t_ch: 有吗？我也不知道您是在将我与谁作比较。
t_en: Really? Then again, I don't know who it is you're comparing me against.

t_ch: …不过，如果我比别的岁阳更好说话，我猜是那艘星槎主人的原因吧。
t_en: ...But if I'm more easy-going than other heliobi, I guess it might be related to the owner of that starskiff...

t_ch: 我是许多无主的小岁阳融聚而成的，自从有意识以来，便有了星槎的记忆。那艘星槎并非斗舰，却曾经在战场上穿梭自如……
t_en: I am the fusion product of several tinier heliobi. Since gaining consciousness, I've had memories of starskiffs. That starskiff is no fighter jet, but long ago it used to zoom across the battlefield...

t_ch: 那艘星槎曾经遭遇致命创伤，龙骨断裂，无法再次执行飞行任务。但是星槎却似乎有着强烈的愿望希望能够再次送上主人一程……
t_en: Once, that starskiff suffered critical damage, snapping its keel beyond all hopes of ever flying again. But the starskiff seemed to have a powerful will of its own, urging it to carry its owner on one last trip...

t_ch: 那艘星槎最后在冲天的火光中焚烧殆尽，只剩下一截龙骨断木被主人从战场上带回，埋在这绥园狐眠冢之下，以寄托哀思。
t_en: That starskiff ultimately went up in flames, with only a section of broken keel salvaged from the battlefield by its owner. It was buried here beneath Fyxestroll Garden Foxsomn Tomb as an expression of grief.

t_ch: 直到我感受到主人的悲伤，却久久没有回到绥园，我才决定出发去找她……
t_en: It wasn't until I felt the owner's sorrow, and yet did not see her returned to Fyxestroll Garden for quite some time, that I decided to set out in search of her...

t_ch: 真是了不起的星槎。
t_en: What an amazing starskiff.

t_ch: 嗯，能听到你这么说，如果星槎有知觉，肯定是十分开心吧。
t_en: Mmm, when I hear you say that, I think that if a starskiff had consciousness, it'd be very happy indeed.

t_ch: …可惜，她已经不再飞翔了。
t_en: ...Unfortunately, she is no longer sky-worthy.

t_ch: 我了解她，我也理解她，我会尊重她的想法。
t_en: I know her, I understand her, and I respect her wishes.

t_ch: 您就算继续问我，我也没有什么可讲的故事了。在此，向您告别。
t_en: You can keep on asking, but I won't have any more stories to tell. That's all for now, farewell.

t_ch: 棋鬼。
t_en: Chess Ghost.

t_ch: 怎么是你？
t_en: It's you!?

t_ch: 怎么老是你啊？
t_en: Why is it always you?

t_ch: 我好像在镇妖塔外面见过你…
t_en: I think I saw you outside the suppression towers...

t_ch: 十王司认定我好像没什么危害性，允许我在这附近溜达溜达。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission determined me to be harmless, so they let me wander around this area.

t_ch: 想来，这是我的特权吧，哈哈哈哈。
t_en: Come to think of it, this is a real privilege of mine. Haha!

t_ch: 是我，你有什么不满意的吗？
t_en: It's me. Have you got anything you're not happy with?

t_ch: 是你，我很满意。
t_en: It's you. I'm very happy.

t_ch: 我与你棋逢对手，是一生的宿敌，当然满意了。
t_en: You and I are evenly-matched rivals, lifelong nemeses... Of course I'm happy.

t_ch: 说起来，我之前确实附身过一个棋盘，好像是叫…仙舟星阵棋？
t_en: Speaking of which, I really did take possession of a chessboard before. I think it was called... Xianzhou starchess?

t_ch: 那个规则太复杂了，我每次想要去理解它的时候，我的意识就不知道到哪里神游去了。
t_en: The rules were byzantine. Every time I tried to learn, my consciousness would just float off somewhere else.

t_ch: …后来，这个棋盘被一位臭屁小孩捡到了，他讨厌学习，对什么事情都不热衷。这可怎么行啊，我便从棋盘上显现，要求这位少年好好学习下棋！
t_en: ...Later on, the chessboard was picked up by this snotty little brat who hated school and who wasn't showing any interest in much at all. This would not do — I appeared from out of the chessboard, and asked this kid to knuckle down and learn some chess!

t_ch: 你有什么本事教别人下棋？
t_en: What talent do you have for teaching a kid chess?

t_ch: 我们交手这么多次，你还不明白吗，我的宿敌。
t_en: We've locked horns so many times, my nemesis. Hasn't it clicked for you yet?

t_ch: 你有什么资格教别人下棋？
t_en: What credentials do you have for teaching a kid chess?

t_ch: 不允许你质疑我的资格，我的宿敌。
t_en: I won't permit you to cast aspersions on my credentials, my nemesis.

t_ch: 你这岁阳还怪好嘞。
t_en: You're pretty good for a heliobus.

t_ch: 嘿嘿。
t_en: Heehee.

t_ch: 但是吧，这个少年曾经一度被称为「天才棋手」，但是后来吧，他一生气就把我依附的棋盘给扔了…说是「跟臭棋篓子下棋越下越臭」。
t_en: However, this kid was soon being called a "chess prodigy." But later, when he lost his temper, he threw out the chessboard that I was inside... He said "playing chess with a real stinker is making things too stinky".

t_ch: 首先排除「我是臭棋篓子」，那么他到底在说谁呢？
t_en: First off, ruling out me as this "stinker" of course, who else could he have been talking about?

t_ch: 我也不知道。
t_en: I don't know either.

t_ch: 你也不知道，我也不知道，真是奇了怪了。
t_en: You don't know. I don't know. It's a real stumper.

t_ch: 他难道不是在说…你？
t_en: There's no way he's talking about... you?

t_ch: 这不可能，你还不了解我吗？
t_en: Impossible! You don't get me, do you?

t_ch: 聊这么多废话，不如来找我下棋，真是的。
t_en: All this gossip. Why, we should be playing chess.

t_ch: 离开镇妖塔。
t_en: Leave the suppression tower.

t_ch: 镇妖塔轻轻浮起，你想要与哪位岁阳对话？
t_en: The suppression towers float gently. Which heliobus would you like to talk to?

t_ch: 犀焰。
t_en: Keralum.

t_ch: 我瞧见了…我瞧见你的未来了。
t_en: I saw it... I saw your future.

t_ch: 你不是来释放我的，你只是来这儿浪费身为短生种宝贵的光阴。
t_en: You're not here to release me, are you? You just want to waste that precious short-life species time of yours.

t_ch: 别唠叨了。
t_en: Shut up.

t_ch: 我代表十王司来审问你…
t_en: I am here on behalf of the Ten-Lords Commission to interrogate you...

t_ch: 只是闲聊罢了，人类，不必说得好似来办什么正事一样。
t_en: This is just small talk, human. Don't take it for anything serious.

t_ch: 不管怎样，我都能看见你一成不变的未来。你会登上一条巨大的铁龙，飞向虚空。
t_en: No matter what, I can see your repetitive future. You'll board a giant iron dragon and fly across the void.

t_ch: 我能看到你的死亡随之降临。就像我预言了新伯利恒的太阳被焚风点燃，我看到奥古尔星环的人类像崩溃蚁群般毫无意义地死去，我还看到了……
t_en: I can see your impending doom. Just as I prophesied that New Bethlehem's sun would be ignited by Zephyro; just as I saw the humans of the Ogul ring die senselessly like an imploding ant colony; so too do I see...

t_ch: 我看到一位神明崛起，在星海中播撒种子——
t_en: I see the rise of a god, who scatters seeds throughout the stars...

t_ch: 这预测说的都是废话。
t_en: This prediction is a load of bull.

t_ch: 是啊，是啊。在你眼中是废话，在岁阳看来却是真实不虚的预测。
t_en: Sure, sure. It's all nonsense in your eyes, but to a heliobus, it's a perfectly sensible, true prediction.

t_ch: 只是你我的时间尺度截然不同，我于一千多年前见证的未来，落在你眼中已成了既成的事实。
t_en: It's just that you operate on a vastly different timescale to me. The future that I first foresaw a millennium ago has grown to become a present that you take for granted.

t_ch: 太卜都算不出我的未来…
t_en: The Master Diviner can't even predict my future...

t_ch: 因为你的颅顶被一片深重的阴影所覆盖。就连岁阳的火光都无法照亮的黑暗。
t_en: Because the top of your skull is enshrouded in a grave fog. This kind of darkness can't be illuminated even by the blaze of a heliobus.

t_ch: 那一定就是你的死亡，你命不久矣。
t_en: It has to be your death. You are not long for this world.

t_ch: 我看透了你的未来，我已说出了你的未来，你知道了你该知道的未来。
t_en: I've seen into your future and I've already told you your future. And you know what you need to do about your future.
